summary: This is an extremely brief placeholder post where Joel Spolsky announces he is going on vacation for a week. He humorously addresses his readers as if they were children being left with a babysitter, telling them to behave. There is no substantive technical or business content. It reflects the casual, personal tone common in early blogging culture where authors maintained a conversational relationship with their audience.
key_points: Joel announces a one-week vacation break from blogging
He uses a playful parent-child metaphor to address his readership
The post illustrates the informal, personal nature of early 2000s blogging culture
money_quotes: Now behave and don't give the babysitter a hard time or I'll dock your allowance.
key_insight: A throwaway vacation notice that demonstrates the personal, conversational relationship early bloggers maintained with their regular readers.
